plan.rationale: Enabled per manifest test_types: unit and security. The crate is a Rust library with embedded unit tests in src/lib.rs; no integration/tests directory exists. Plan: 1) Unit: run cargo test --all-targets (timeout 60s) to execute #[cfg(test)] in src/lib.rs covering average, is_valid_email, clamp, fibonacci, gcd. Based on current code comments, expect failures in test_email_invalid_no_at and test_clamp_above_max; the #[should_panic] average empty test should pass by panicking. 2) Security: static analysis focusing on correctness and potential panics. Run cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features (advisory; timeout 120s) and semgrep --config p/ci --lang=rust src (timeout 120s). Optionally, run cargo +nightly miri test if environment permits, to detect UB in tests. Results should be collected into the artifacts directory as configured by the harness.

findings.recommendation: Exit 101 suggests the test binary panicked or had failing assertions. Reproduce locally (e.g., run: cargo test -- --nocapture and narrow to the failing test), inspect the logs/backtrace, fix the underlying logic or adjust test expectations, and remove any flakiness (timeouts, nondeterminism, reliance on system state).

Miri exit 1 typically indicates UB was detected. Run locally with Miri to get the full report (e.g., cargo miri test with -Zmiri-strict-provenance, -Zmiri-tag-raw-pointers, and RUST_BACKTRACE=1). Fix the unsafe code causing UB (invalid deref, out-of-bounds, use-after-free, uninitialized read, or violated aliasing/Send/Sync invariants), add targeted tests, and prefer safe abstractions to avoid the unsafe pattern.

summary: Ran 4 tests across 1 languages (rust). Tools: cargo clippy, cargo miri, cargo test, semgrep. 2 passed, 2 failed, 0 healed. Findings: 1 critical, 1 high
